166884.explanation: Referendums reduce complex decisions to a yes or no choice and do not do justice to the complexity of proposals. Referendums increase and sharpen divisions instead of creating a solution many people agree with. Councillors are representatives who take decisions.

166886.explanation: Healthy municipal finances mean a balanced and responsible budget. Over the next four years, we aim not to raise property tax by making tough choices, working efficiently, and managing community funds responsibly.

166887.explanation: Cooperation with other municipalities can lead to better quality and lower costs. Where clear benefits are achievable, we consciously choose cooperation. At the same time, the municipality retains control so that local interests, say, and democratic oversight remain guaranteed.

166888.explanation: A complete ban on expansion goes too far. Building must remain possible within permit limits, but CDA wants to focus on good consultation with farmers, with room for innovation, goal orientation, and transition support. This way we combine a liveable landscape with prospects for farmers.

166891.explanation: As CDA, we want to stimulate tourism in Achtkarspelen by increasing overnight stays and improving recreational facilities.

166893.explanation: Achtkarspelen has relatively much natural land and national rural NNN tasks have been completed. The area process Achtkarspelen-South is coming to an end, so there is no need for extra nature. Farmers remain owners of their land and responsible for decisions about sale or new use.

166895.explanation: As CDA, we support sustainability and are happy to think along about good integration of energy projects. However, we believe that energy cooperatives must operate financially independently.

166896.explanation: As CDA, we oppose the placement of high-voltage masts in our municipality. We already submitted a motion on this in 2025.

166897.explanation: CDA stands for a liveable landscape where safety, support, and biodiversity are central. Achtkarspelen is not a suitable habitat for wolves; continuous nature areas are. When a wolf causes problems, the municipality must urge the province to cull it.

166898.explanation: Clustering of shops strengthens the shopping area. Concentrating shops creates a lively center.

166899.explanation: Taking good care of the earth is essential, for ourselves and future generations. Stewardship is central to CDA, which is why it remains important that the municipality supports and encourages sustainability.

166900.explanation: The goal is to make as many homes as possible more energy-efficient, regardless of income. People with a low income often live in less well-insulated houses and therefore benefit relatively more from support.

166901.explanation: Provided this complies with laws and regulations.

166902.explanation: To strengthen social cohesion in the community, CDA wants to provide financial support to residents’ initiatives through local interests.

166903.explanation: According to CDA, solar panels belong on roofs of homes or business buildings. Agricultural land plays an important role in food production and must be preserved for that purpose.

166904.explanation: It is important that there are sufficient facilities for everyone, with attention to people with disabilities.

166905.explanation: Safety and biodiversity are important, so mowing should be decided on a case-by-case basis regarding where and when it can happen. At the same time, municipal greenery should look neat and well-maintained.

166906.explanation: Everyone matters and is respected. The use of the rainbow symbol is not necessary for that.

166907.explanation: We do not want to give priority but do want to fulfill our legal obligation.

166908.explanation: We want to fulfill our legal obligation.

166909.explanation: In existing integration programs, it is important that newcomers also get the chance to get to know the municipality of Achtkarspelen, its villages, residents, and local customs.

166910.explanation: This hardly occurs in Achtkarspelen. Housing seasonal workers on their own company grounds is not permitted.

166911.explanation: Music and other creative subjects deserve support because they stimulate children’s general development in an important way.

166912.explanation: Good education requires a minimum number of students and teachers. Schools must be able to function with quality and responsibility.

166913.explanation: It’s about quality and accessibility; clustering is not an end in itself. Education, childcare, and after-school activities must be well accessible for every child.

166914.explanation: The viability of sports clubs is important. By abolishing the rent for sports fields, space is created to pay the clubs’ insurance premiums.

166915.explanation: For this, the schools have their own budget.

166916.explanation: People with disabilities should be able to fully participate in society. Additional fishing spots make recreation accessible to everyone.

166917.explanation: As the CDA, we find it important that everyone has access to books.

166918.explanation: Multilingualism is beneficial for the development of children. Lessons in both Dutch and Frisian as well as world languages contribute to broad language skills and cultural knowledge.

166919.explanation: A good distribution of facilities is important. Schools, village halls, and sports fields should be present, provided there is sufficient support through active use.

166920.explanation: Residents must be protected online. The CDA wants education about cybercrime to counteract fraud and digital scams.

166921.explanation: Chains must not cause nuisance. Therefore, they must be registered with the municipality.

166922.explanation: Proper enforcement is important for safety in the municipality.

166924.explanation: Continuous awareness campaigns are important to make young people aware of the risks of alcohol, drugs, and other addictions such as gambling.

166925.explanation: People need to feel safe. Camera surveillance in appropriate locations can prevent nuisance and therefore deserves extra attention.

166926.explanation: Both Dutch and Frisian place names are appropriate. The use of both languages is sufficient and does not need to be limited to one official name.

166927.explanation: The CDA focuses on structural housing construction that provides full-fledged homes and creates real solutions for those seeking housing. Temporary housing units do not solve the structural shortage because residents will need a regular home on the market after a certain period.

166929.explanation: Housing corporations are responsible for the management and allocation of social rental homes.

166930.explanation: The CDA believes housing should be built for all target groups, tailored to actual demand. It’s not about a fixed percentage but a balanced distribution that fits the needs of the residents.

166931.explanation: New construction must be prepared for extreme weather conditions. Good insulation and water drainage.

166932.explanation: If existing housing corporations do not realize enough social rental homes, the municipality can consider establishing its own housing corporation.

166933.explanation: It is practically not possible to enforce this generally. Only in possible new construction can it be evaluated per case whether a self-occupancy obligation is desirable. However, the most important thing is that there are enough homes where people actually live.

166934.explanation: Building standards are necessary to ensure that construction and renovation are done neatly and orderly. Without rules, the buildings become messy and incoherent.

166935.explanation: The CDA believes this should mainly be addressed by the market. The municipality can facilitate this.

166936.explanation: According to the CDA, the municipality should support residents who have difficulties, for example with high energy costs or other financial challenges, so that everyone can participate in society.

166937.explanation: If possible, a reciprocal obligation can contribute to social participation. For people who cannot do this, for example due to health or other circumstances, there is of course no obligation.

166938.explanation: Subsidized jobs are important to help the long-term unemployed reintegrate into the labor market. The focus should be maximally on offering opportunities and guidance towards regular jobs.

166939.explanation: We want people to actively work and seize opportunities to independently provide for their own income. Compliance with the job application requirement helps with this.

166940.explanation: More opportunities to earn extra help people gain experience and provide a good flow towards a regular job.

166942.explanation: The consequences of such a development are difficult to estimate. Therefore, we will not take a position for now and first examine the possible impact.

166944.explanation: The CDA believes that especially important cycling routes should be prioritized and well lit. Not every cycle path needs to be fully lit.

166945.explanation: We find it important that there are enough charging points.

166947.explanation: Fast and safe bicycle connections link Achtkarspelen with surrounding cities and villages. This is best realized in collaboration with other municipalities.

166949.explanation: The station forms an important connection with Groningen and Leeuwarden. Many students and workers use it daily, so the municipality must urge Arriva to include express train stops.

166950.explanation: Looking out for each other in the villages is valuable. Through village interests, the municipality can support and stimulate initiatives so that residents continue to actively help each other.

166951.explanation: Meeting places for the elderly are important for social contacts and well-being. The CDA believes the municipality should look together with local interests at the possibilities.

166952.explanation: Preventive youth care at schools helps identify and address problems early. Area team employees can support students so that they can develop.

166954.explanation: The amount of the personal contribution is set nationally and cannot be adjusted by the municipality.

166955.explanation: In youth care, the interests of the young people are central. Quality of care always takes precedence over price.

166884.explanation: To gauge residents' opinions, a referendum can be a good instrument.

166886.explanation: The principle is to keep the property tax at the current level. The property tax may be indexed based on inflation.

166887.explanation: The FNP is in favor of cooperation where possible, adds value, saves costs, and ensures the influence of municipal councils and residents.

166888.explanation: Expansion of intensive livestock farming should not mean an increase in the number of animals, but an increase in living space that improves animal welfare.

166891.explanation: We believe the municipality should showcase our unique landscape to tourists.

166893.explanation: We strive for a good balance between agriculture, recreation, nature, landscape, and livability.

166895.explanation: It is not the municipality's task to invest in an energy collective.

166896.explanation: The high-voltage masts are an irrevocable impairment of nature, the landscape, ecology, and the quality of living in our municipality.

166897.explanation: In a large part of Achtkarspelen, considering the wildlife population and the presence of people and livestock, there is no sustainable place for the establishment of wolves. Active management of the wolf is necessary in these areas. The safety of people and (farm) animals must always come first.

166898.explanation: It is the entrepreneur’s freedom to establish themselves in the location they choose.

166899.explanation: As long as the municipality is sufficiently compensated for the stated objectives without having to make municipal investments ourselves, we can continue with these arrangements.

166900.explanation: The current criteria for the 'Smûk en Sunich' project are good.

166901.explanation: This is possible if the owner uses the generated energy themselves.

166902.explanation: Plans must always be supported, provided they are good enough.

166903.explanation: We believe there are still enough roofs available that can be used for the same purpose.

166904.explanation: The toilets must be accessible not only to people with disabilities but to everyone who needs them.

166905.explanation: The way it is arranged now is fine.

166906.explanation: We believe that everyone is equal and that we are open to all. This can also be done without the designation 'rainbow municipality'.

166907.explanation: We oppose continuing to give priority to refugees and status holders when allocating social rental housing. This is unfair to people who have also been waiting for a house for years. We support giving priority to people with a socio-economic connection to the municipality of Achtkarspelen or one of its villages.

166908.explanation: The FNP does not want asylum seekers’ centers (AZCs). We choose small-scale reception in the villages where there is support and space. This way, we maintain a human scale and liveability, with emphasis on rapid integration through language, work, and the local population.

166909.explanation: New residents of the municipality must be aware of the history and culture of the area.

166910.explanation: The livability of this housing must be carefully considered.

166911.explanation: Although creative subjects, such as general musical education, are important for the development of a child, it is not the municipality’s task to organize this after school hours. A lot of attention is already given to these subjects during school hours.

166912.explanation: It is crucial to keep facilities such as primary schools in our villages, provided the quality of education is guaranteed.

166913.explanation: When there is room to develop a site where all these facilities can be located, this is, in the FNP’s view, a good starting point.

166914.explanation: Not all insurances of the sports clubs can be covered by the municipality.

166915.explanation: This is a task for education.

166916.explanation: Fishing is a fully recognized sport and we must ensure that our residents can practice it, even if they have a disability.

166917.explanation: If it concerns a lending point of the North-East Friesland Library, this is not possible.

166918.explanation: Good multilingual education is essential for the development of all children.

166919.explanation: If there is a need for these facilities and it is sustainable to maintain them, we must do everything to preserve them.

166920.explanation: We are more vulnerable than ever to digital crime; it is important to warn everyone about this.

166921.explanation: We believe that the locations of the chains should be transparent and that they meet safety requirements.

166922.explanation: Depending on the evaluation of the current enforcers, more may be needed.

166924.explanation: Besides the municipality, education and the national government must also do this. It is important to communicate this through multiple channels.

166925.explanation: In places with an increased risk, this must be possible.

166926.explanation: The official names of the villages must be used.

166927.explanation: These temporary homes must meet the applicable requirements and be reported to the municipality.

166929.explanation: It is important to offer housing seekers with a social or economic interest the opportunity to live in the municipality.

166930.explanation: The ratio of new homes to be built must not be changed. Construction should be based on the needs in the villages.

166931.explanation: If extreme weather occurs, we must be prepared for it.

166932.explanation: There are already housing corporations active in the municipality; they are responsible for this.

166933.explanation: This promotes social cohesion and mutual bonding, which increases safety and livability.

166934.explanation: Aesthetic regulations are important for cohesion in the environment.

166935.explanation: It is not a municipal task to facilitate this.

166936.explanation: There are already many arrangements that prevent people from living in poverty. In addition, we will need to focus more on early detection.

166937.explanation: We believe that residents who receive support from the municipality should also make a positive contribution to society.

166938.explanation: Participating in society is important for every individual.

166939.explanation: The obligation to apply for jobs should only apply to residents who are capable of doing so.

166940.explanation: The current rules used by the municipality are good.

166942.explanation: It is not advisable to create a pond, but it is a good idea to establish a waterway: 'The Lits-Lauwersmeer route.'

166944.explanation: Illuminated bicycle paths reduce the risk of accidents by improving visibility of obstacles, other road users, and gaps in the road.

166945.explanation: Designating these spots must not come at the expense of existing parking spaces and should therefore only be an expansion there.

166947.explanation: We are in favor of fast bicycle connections. Achtkarspelen must urge the province to establish this because it is not solely the responsibility of our municipality.

166949.explanation: The train station in Buitenpost serves an important regional function. Therefore, it is important that travelers can continue to use the connections. This issue should also be raised with the province.

166950.explanation: What is undertaken by village communities themselves is wonderful and, of course, it is useful for the municipality to encourage this. An example is the initiative from the deaconries of Surhuisterveen, namely the project 'Together at the Table,' which organizes monthly meals for people with limited means or who are lonely and deserves support.

166951.explanation: In addition to the elderly, there is also a need for meeting places for other target groups; these should also be facilitated.

166952.explanation: The approach of having an area team worker at school works well. Early detection is crucial; prevention is better than cure.

166954.explanation: Although this is national policy, we believe the contribution should be income-dependent.

166955.explanation: Price is important, but other factors also play a role in choosing the right organization, such as quality and reliability.

166884.explanation: It is important that residents, organizations, and businesses are well involved in matters that concern them. However, we do not think a referendum is the right tool for this because achieving a good and representative turnout is often difficult.

166886.explanation: If a financial shortfall threatens, we will first try to compensate by cutting costs. We will do our utmost to protect people in dependent, vulnerable situations in case of cuts. A tax increase is only considered as a last resort.

166887.explanation: The municipality is receiving increasingly more and also more complex tasks. To perform these well and keep implementation affordable for residents, cooperation is necessary.

166888.explanation: The future of livestock farming and all related regulations is primarily a matter for the national government. We do not see it as necessary or wise to be stricter or less strict than national policy.

166891.explanation: Recently, the Fryske Wâlden were designated as an Icon Landscape of the Netherlands. This offers economic opportunities. We want to invest in attractive green and recreational facilities and stimulate forms of recreational accommodation in and around Achtkarspelen in cooperation with entrepreneurs.

166893.explanation: Converting agricultural land into nature is almost always a matter for the province, not the municipality. However, we are not fundamentally opposed if agricultural land gets another designation such as nature, housing, or business park.

166895.explanation: Resident initiatives and local energy cooperatives can play an important role in developing wind and solar energy generation. Residents collectively reap the benefits. The municipality can support and encourage this, but direct financial support is not the first thing we think of.

166896.explanation: These masts are not attractive but unfortunately necessary for a stable electricity grid. Opposing them is pointless, wastes money and time, and is therefore not right. We must focus on good compensation and involvement of immediate neighbors and a regional fund.

166897.explanation: Due to lack of natural predators, the wolf population can increase significantly with all undesirable consequences. We believe there is no place for wolves in our cultural landscape. Wolves must be kept out and, if necessary, shot.

166898.explanation: The reality is that retail is changing and under pressure. We focus on compact and attractive shopping areas. Shops in smaller villages also have an important role, and therefore we see no need to implement policies that oppose this.

166899.explanation: We want to prevent further global warming, protect nature, and prepare our living environment for extreme weather. Justice is paramount for us: the transition to a sustainable society can only succeed if it is fair-achievable and affordable for everyone.

166900.explanation: Many sustainability measures lead to savings, and many residents can make investments themselves. In such cases, municipal contributions are not needed. Municipal attention should focus on residents with limited means who often live in less well-insulated homes.

166901.explanation: Wind turbines can be an important source of sustainable energy and locally relieve the overloaded electricity grid. Due to landscape integration, not all locations are suitable.

166902.explanation: In Achtkarspelen, associations, music schools, sports clubs, and churches are places where community is visible. Cultural events also belong to this. It is good if the municipality supports certain activities with subsidies. Clear frameworks are important here.

166903.explanation: The municipality of Achtkarspelen works with the solar ladder. This means that in the first instance, solar panels on roofs are considered and only lastly on agricultural land. We think this is a fine principle.

166904.explanation: Insufficient public toilets are a problem for quite a few people. Think of seniors, parents with children, or people with illnesses. We can address this by, for example, opening toilets in municipal buildings and through consultation with hospitality and entrepreneurs.

166905.explanation: In Achtkarspelen, management of public green spaces strongly considers nature values and biodiversity. We think this is a good thing, and where possible, we should take an extra step. Currently, we see no concrete options regarding public lawns.

166906.explanation: We recognize the societal problems experienced by the LGBTIQA+ group, but they are unfortunately not unique in this. Think of other minorities in society such as people of different skin colors, refugees, people with disabilities, Jews, etc. The municipality is there for everyone.

166907.explanation: Priority is necessary because municipalities are obliged to house them, which is needed for rapid integration (language, work, school) and to relieve overcrowded asylum centers. They constitute a small share of the total number of homes released annually. Building more homes is the real solution.

166908.explanation: Every municipality has a responsibility to accommodate and distribute asylum seekers. This way, we can take responsibility for fellow humans who are fleeing and awaiting the outcome of their asylum request. The asylum problem itself must be solved at the European level.

166909.explanation: A local integration course could, in our view, be broader than just Achtkarspelen, but we think newcomers can be asked to become part of our society and integrate as quickly as possible.

166910.explanation: In other parts of the country, this problem occurs more than in Achtkarspelen, but where it does exist, there must be an end to distressing living conditions and exploitation of labor migrants, such as wage deductions or homelessness after losing their job.

166911.explanation: For young people, it is important that accessible offerings exist so they can discover their creative talents. These are valuable ways to develop talents and collaborate, but making this completely free for everyone, regardless of financial situation, is not necessary.

166912.explanation: This is a task of the national government. The financial resources for this must therefore come from the national government. The municipality can lobby for this, but it must be prevented that the municipality ends up paying for national government responsibilities.

166913.explanation: Preferably, children should be able to attend daycare, school, sports, and cultural activities in their own village. However, this is not always realistic. Where possible, the municipality facilitates this in the Integrated Housing Plan for schools.

166914.explanation: By collective arrangements, this is cheaper. This relieves sports clubs and ensures insurance coverage. It also relieves volunteers. This will become part of the new sports policy.

166915.explanation: School vegetable gardens teach children how to grow food and raise awareness about healthy nutrition. This is organized externally so that schools and parents are not burdened with extra tasks.

166916.explanation: Public spaces are designed to be accessible and allow people with disabilities to navigate independently. This also applies to sports and cultural facilities, public buildings, and municipal events.

166917.explanation: Reading and access to information must be available to everyone. In every village there should be an option for a lending point in a school or community center.

166918.explanation: Language development in children contributes more broadly to overall development. Promoting early multilingualism has proven benefits for development.

166919.explanation: Meeting places, education, and sports must be available in every village. However, schools are subject to national regulations over which the municipality has no influence. The municipality nevertheless strives for this through the Integrated Housing Plan and lobbying the national government.

166920.explanation: Cybercrime is increasing rapidly. We need to be aware of it and become resilient. Through education, we prevent our residents and businesses from becoming victims and possibly facing problems.

166921.explanation: Young people should be able to meet in small-scale, safe, and legal ways. Non-commercial venues are allowed, provided they comply with existing laws and regulations regarding safety and substance use. A reporting obligation ensures proper guidance.

166922.explanation: Supervision and enforcement increase the safety of our residents. We invest in good relations with young people, focus on preventing drug and alcohol use, and detecting and preventing undermining and crime. We increase visibility and availability for our residents.

166924.explanation: We want the municipality to do more against normalizing substance use. Among other things, by providing more information about the consequences of drug use for parents and young people, stricter enforcement of age limits, and banning alcohol and gambling advertising in public spaces.

166925.explanation: Camera surveillance increases safety and prevents crime in places such as nightlife areas and locations where nuisance is experienced. However, it must not infringe on people's privacy; camera placement must be done carefully and only when necessary.

166926.explanation: We promote the Frisian language, but funds can only be spent once. In case of high costs, such as prematurely replacing road signs, we choose other priorities.

166927.explanation: The housing shortage is severe and calls for quick measures. A temporary home for young people or elderly on their own property also boosts social cohesion and looking out for each other.

166929.explanation: Many young people want to remain living in their own village. This is important for the village. Therefore, we must primarily build more homes and avoid rigidly allocating the small number of available rental homes to specific groups. Legally, giving priority to young people in our municipality is not possible.

166930.explanation: Building homes in all price ranges ensures a healthy mix of affordable and more expensive houses that match the housing desires in our municipality. At least 30% should be affordable rental housing and 30% affordable owner-occupied housing.

166931.explanation: In construction projects and public space design, climate adaptation is the standard. Squares receive solutions to drain and store water through wadi's without compromising accessibility. We increase the sponge effect of existing streets by adding more greenery.

166932.explanation: The municipality is not a property developer. However, it must more strictly assess performance agreements with housing corporations. Additionally, the municipality can stimulate housing construction, actively manage land policy, and support initiatives where needed and desirable.

166933.explanation: Landlords can play a role in solving the housing shortage. However, this must be regulated to prevent (excessively) high rents. A regular home must be permanently occupied and may not be used as a second home or for recreational rental, for example.

166934.explanation: Aesthetic regulations ensure that buildings in our villages fit together, traditional village appearances are preserved, and the look is not marred by a few deviant buildings. However, it is worthwhile to assess if certain requirements can be removed or simplified.

166935.explanation: Vacancy is a waste for both home seekers and the appearance of a building or street. However, it must be an appropriate location. Industrial areas are not always suitable, for example. The municipality must support as much as possible but be cautious about investing its own money here.

166936.explanation: We help residents as much as possible to find suitable work. We take measures against poverty such as individual income supplements, support initiatives against poverty, and provide local tailored assistance for high energy bills and rent arrears. We offer financial education and budget coaching.

166937.explanation: Where possible, we expect a contribution to society and an active attitude to become self-reliant as soon as possible. Sometimes, it is not possible to provide a reciprocal service, for example a working mother with income supplementation or in cases of illness and disability. There is understanding for that.

166938.explanation: Working pays off. Not only financially but also personally. Daily activities and gaining experience are important pillars to become work-ready again. Participation in society does not always come naturally; therefore, we gladly offer support to become self-reliant.

166939.explanation: The principle of earning your own income is leading. When that is not possible, help is available to become self-reliant again through job coaching, subsidized jobs, and learning places. We apply the Simple Switching method where stepping back temporarily is allowed to progress later.

166940.explanation: The principle of earning your own income is leading. Earnings above the social assistance standard are deducted from welfare benefits when this is structural and does not lead to a full-fledged job where one becomes financially independent again.

166942.explanation: We are not against it but see no role for the municipality in the development.

166944.explanation: Light pollution plays a major role. Where it is unsafe, we consider (LED) lighting with motion sensors.

166945.explanation: We advocate for smart charging stations so that electric cars can serve as buffers in the electricity grid.

166947.explanation: We use the STOMP principle (first walking, cycling, public transport, Mobility as a Service and only then private car). Better for health, wallet, and environment.

166949.explanation: For accessibility and livability of our municipality, it is important that Buitenpost station continues to be served.

166950.explanation: Informal caregivers are becoming increasingly important in the fight against loneliness. They are invaluable for the well-being of our residents, preventing care, and keeping our society livable.

166951.explanation: Meeting each other is the foundation of looking out for one another and very important for social cohesion. Moreover, it combats loneliness.

166952.explanation: The youth worker and the area team worker in schools have proven their value. They promote the general well-being of young people and thus indirectly their families. This prevents problems and reduces youth care needs.

166954.explanation: An income-dependent contribution for high incomes is reasonable to us. However, income-dependent contributions should not lead to disproportionate stacking of healthcare costs for the chronically ill.

166955.explanation: Quality of care comes first, but municipal spending has limits. Care fraudsters are tackled and excluded from municipal procurement. Care is not a business model but a shared social task.

166884.explanation: Residents of the municipality must be involved in major decisions made within the municipality. This participation and/or consultation does not need to be via a referendum.

166886.explanation: We strive for affordable housing in our municipality.

166887.explanation: Achtkarspelen remains an independent municipality. We focus on cooperation with neighboring municipalities and the province to jointly advance on matters such as infrastructure, education, and care.

166888.explanation: Where possible, no expansion of intensive livestock farming. Existing farms may remain.

166891.explanation: Funds for small-scale campsites and expanding our cycling connections.

166893.explanation: There is already a lot of nature area in Achtkarspelen. Agricultural land must also remain available.

166895.explanation: Sustainability is important, but it must also remain feasible for residents and entrepreneurs. We believe in practical solutions that work for our region, without unrealistic demands.

166897.explanation: If the wolf causes many problems and damage, the municipality can submit a request to the province to resolve it.

166898.explanation: In smaller villages, a shop is also important.

166899.explanation: Small-scale and local projects that actually contribute to a better environment and a stronger economy should be encouraged.

166900.explanation: Must be available to everyone who needs it.

166901.explanation: Yes, large wind turbines are not permitted in Achtkarspelen.

166902.explanation: Good plans can always be encouraged in consultation with the municipality.

166903.explanation: There are still plenty of empty roofs in Achtkarspelen, or cover parking lots.

166904.explanation: The municipality should encourage entrepreneurs to make their toilets available. Possibly for a reasonable fee.

166905.explanation: For some locations that is not a problem and it looks nice, but this is not desirable or safe everywhere.

166906.explanation: Achtkarspelen must be a safe environment for everyone.

166907.explanation: No, all housing seekers have equal rights.

166908.explanation: In our small villages, shelters are often not desired. Refugees should be accommodated according to nature and scale, so in larger centers.

166909.explanation: Integration courses simply belong to the Netherlands and the municipality. It is mandatory.

166911.explanation: Not everything can be free. Depends on subsidies.

166912.explanation: If there are other primary schools nearby, cooperation should be encouraged. Still strive to have a school in every village.

166913.explanation: Every shelter may have its own place.

166914.explanation: Up to a certain level, but the municipality cannot pay for everything.

166915.explanation: A school can organize that itself.

166916.explanation: In our municipality there are many places for people with disabilities to fish.

166917.explanation: Not necessary. There is a library in Surhuisterveen and Buitenpost, and books are also available at schools.

166918.explanation: These three languages are an important foundation.

166919.explanation: Schools, sports facilities, and village halls are the glue of the community and important for the development and connection of young and old.

166920.explanation: Not a task of the municipality.

166921.explanation: There is chain policy in Achtkarspelen.

166924.explanation: Collaboration with schools is important. Area teams have a nice role in this.

166925.explanation: Not necessary. Privacy is also important.

166927.explanation: Temporary homes on the property are possible, but only with permission from the municipality.

166929.explanation: Yes, many residents want to continue living in their own familiar place.

166931.explanation: All houses built nowadays must meet these requirements.

166932.explanation: We have SWA for that. Or other entrepreneurs.

166933.explanation: Yes, to prevent abuse and for a fair distribution.

166934.explanation: Not all rules can be abolished, but too many rules sometimes make building impossible.

166935.explanation: The municipality can encourage owners to convert their vacant properties into homes.

166936.explanation: It depends on how one falls into poverty. Help with money management is needed.

166937.explanation: Everyone who has the opportunity should consider doing volunteer work.

166939.explanation: There is still plenty of work that needs to be done.

166940.explanation: You receive benefits because you are unable to participate in the labor market.

166942.explanation: There is plenty of water in Friesland; adding more does not add value.

166944.explanation: Always; it can also be done with small LED lights.

166945.explanation: More and more people are using electric cars, but not everyone has a driveway to charge their vehicle.

166949.explanation: If every train has to stop there, it’s no longer an express train.

166950.explanation: Not everything can be solved with money.

166951.explanation: Loneliness among the elderly does exist. A meeting place is important for social contacts.

166952.explanation: The municipality spends enough money on preventive youth care in schools. GIS employees (Area Team In School).

166955.explanation: Price and quality must be in a good balance, which means the municipality must continue to monitor costs and the care provided. If desired, the contract with the youth care provider can be terminated when quality and price relationship do not match.

166884.explanation: As residents, we have a say over our own municipality. When an issue is very prominent, a referendum can be a fair and effective way to make a decision. However, issues are often too complex for a referendum. We would prefer to use citizens’ assemblies more often.

166886.explanation: Increasing taxes or charges is never the first choice, so preferably not. It must be clear what the extra money is used for.

166887.explanation: We face major challenges. These do not respect municipal boundaries. Therefore, we need each other to address these challenges. Think, for example, of labor market shortages and complex issues in youth care.

166888.explanation: The expansion of intensive animal farming endangers the future of our living environment. It causes more emissions and harms nature and health. The municipality must choose a sustainable living environment and therefore prevent further expansion.

166891.explanation: We would like to have more accommodation options in our municipality. To make the municipality more attractive, we want a well-connected walking and cycling path network.

166893.explanation: Nature is of great importance for the future of our planet and thus for our municipality.

166895.explanation: Sustainable energy is the future; we are eager to promote it.

166896.explanation: The placement of high-voltage pylons is not a municipal task. Nobody wants them in their municipality, but we all need them. Governments, grid operators, and residents must take joint responsibility and seek the best option for everyone.

166897.explanation: The wolf has returned and we do not keep it out at borders. We must learn to live with the wolf by properly protecting livestock and responsibly dealing with wild animals as humans.

166898.explanation: The market has already clustered shops itself. Shops in small villages are great for livability, but ultimately it is the market that decides. Residents can influence this by shopping locally.

166899.explanation: For a sustainable municipality, we all must live more sustainably. As a municipality, we must set a good example and help our residents make this possible.

166900.explanation: We find it important that everyone is eligible to make their home more sustainable.

166901.explanation: Where possible, we must encourage sustainability. Moreover, various forms of sustainable energy help solve problems related to grid congestion.

166902.explanation: To strengthen the community feeling, we want to establish a 'Mei-inoar Fûns' (Together Fund). A low-threshold municipal fund where residents, associations, and village interests can jointly submit plans that contribute to meeting, cooperation, and livability.

166903.explanation: A field full of solar panels can provide many households with clean, affordable energy. If farmers want, they should be able to use their land for this. Moreover, solar panels can be well combined with more biodiversity, for example by growing flowers and other low greenery.

166904.explanation: We support more public toilets, but want them to be accessible to everyone who needs them.

166905.explanation: Long and flourishing grass is good for biodiversity. Still, we find it important to take traffic safety into account.

166906.explanation: A rainbow municipality shows that everyone is welcome. Too many people still do not feel safe or at home in our municipality. By actively promoting inclusion and acceptance, we create an environment where everyone can be themselves.

166907.explanation: They are one of many groups with urgent needs; transition is important. They must leave asylum seeker centers as soon as possible for good integration. We especially need to build more houses to solve the housing shortage.

166908.explanation: We support reception suited to the nature and scale of this municipality. A small-scale asylum seeker center in one of the larger villages is no more than our human duty.

166909.explanation: We consider social guidance very important, for example through a buddy from the municipality itself. A local course does not provide sufficient support.

166910.explanation: People who come to do honest work must be treated with care and attention. Good housing is important.

166911.explanation: Equal opportunities for everyone to discover talents are very important to us. Music and creative subjects stimulate brain development and promote creativity, concentration, and socio-emotional skills.

166912.explanation: We value high-quality education, which is better organized in larger schools. At the same time, we want to invest in good school transportation from smaller villages to larger centers. This helps maintain livability.

166913.explanation: Having everything under one roof creates calmness and coherence for children and increases equal opportunities. Equal chances for every child is one of our long-term goals.

166914.explanation: Collective purchasing of insurance is cheaper for sports clubs and thus also for our residents.

166915.explanation: It is a great idea and educational for students. However, it must be a request from schools. They are responsible for education.

166916.explanation: We find it important that nature and recreation are accessible to all people. However, we also find it important that fishermen take animal welfare and sustainability into account.

166917.explanation: We consider it very important that everyone has access to books. A physical location is not necessary; it could also be a voluntary courier service. They pick up and deliver books on request.

166918.explanation: We support high-quality language education, but this is a responsibility of the education system itself.

166919.explanation: For livability, it is important to maintain as many facilities as possible. Keeping them open is not an end in itself; we support a wide range of social services, but not more of the same.

166920.explanation: The government in general must commit to this, so the municipality must also take responsibility.

166921.explanation: Chains are very important for our youth. We think requiring permission goes too far, but we would like to see a notification obligation. That way, we know where the young people are and can easily make contact with them.

166922.explanation: Rules and agreements have been made in our municipality that we all must follow, but we have few enforcement officers (boa’s) to uphold them.

166924.explanation: Through good information and conversations, you help young people make more conscious choices. It prevents health problems, risky behavior, and addiction. Additionally, it contributes to a safe, healthy, and responsible living environment for youth.

166925.explanation: Camera surveillance can help in problematic areas, but it is not a preventive, structural measure. Our privacy is an important consideration.

166926.explanation: It is a nice ambition, but at the moment both place names are already in use. Changing to Frisian costs extra money for our residents. We would rather invest that money in our community.

166927.explanation: We support building homes on private property, but to prevent uncontrolled growth it is important that this is reported to the municipality. We believe the municipality should do everything to facilitate this quickly. A notification obligation could also be sufficient.

166929.explanation: Research shows that most people who get a house already come from this area. Limiting it to only people from the own municipality means people from surrounding villages no longer have a chance.

166930.explanation: There is a great need for rental homes. To increase the stock of rental homes, 40% of newly built houses must be social rental homes. The stock of social rental homes has drastically shrunk in recent years.

166931.explanation: Climate change is a fact, as are extreme weather conditions. We must prepare for this and take it into account when designing the municipality.

166932.explanation: In recent years, social rental housing was left to the market. Considering the current problems, it is clear the market cannot handle this. Good housing is a basic provision; we as a municipality must take back control.

166933.explanation: Homes are meant to be lived in, not to be bought up for renting out.

166934.explanation: However, we do support more flexible and clearer rules, also to accelerate housing construction. Completely abolishing them could quickly and negatively change the street image.

166935.explanation: We believe the municipality should encourage and facilitate this. There are many opportunities here for rapid housing development.

166936.explanation: Income limits for minimum income schemes in Achtkarspelen have gradually been lowered in recent years, so fewer and fewer residents qualify for these schemes. We want the income limit to be restored to 130% of the social minimum.

166937.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. Extra demands and conditions only create more uncertainty and cause new problems.

166938.explanation: People naturally want to contribute. Suitable work can sometimes be hard to find. We think it is important that a suitable place is found for everyone who wants and can work.

166939.explanation: People are not on welfare because they don’t want to work. They need a stable foundation, affordable housing, and trust from the municipality. A mandatory job application requirement is an unnecessary extra pressure that may cause new problems.

166940.explanation: We believe welfare recipients should be allowed to earn extra income so that working always pays off, people remain independent, and poverty is limited. Additional income provides financial freedom, boosts self-confidence, and encourages steps towards regular employment.

166942.explanation: The strength of Achtkarspelen is precisely the beautiful cycling and walking paths through the charming landscape. We want to focus on promoting this and thus showcase our unique strengths.

166944.explanation: Lighting must have a purpose. Illuminating all bike paths causes a lot of light pollution and is not good for nature and animals. We do find it important that good lighting is available in places where people feel unsafe.

166945.explanation: As more electric vehicles appear, the demand for charging points also increases. We as a municipality must facilitate this growing demand.

166947.explanation: We consider a good cycling path network very important. Safe cycling paths to the large surrounding centers encourage bicycle use.

166949.explanation: As soon as the opportunity arises, we will always fight hard for this.

166950.explanation: We need each other to keep the villages livable. There will always be a call on the surroundings of people who need help. The municipality can play a connecting role here.

166951.explanation: Meeting places help elderly people stay socially active. They offer a place for contact, activities, and support, prevent loneliness, and ensure the elderly remain involved and connected to the community.

166952.explanation: By offering early help to young people, larger problems can be prevented. School is the place where young people are visible and where problems often come to light early. By providing early and low-threshold help here, intensive care needs can be avoided.

166954.explanation: By keeping care income-dependent, it remains fair and accessible. People with low income get the help they need, while higher incomes can bear the costs themselves without unduly burdening their financial situation.

166955.explanation: The waiting lists and problems in youth assistance and youth care are enormous. You don’t solve this by cutting more and always choosing the cheapest options. We must especially ensure certainty and provide help that matches the concerns of our youth.

166884.explanation: PVV wants real decision-making power for residents; for major decisions (such as Ukrainian refugee reception) binding representative influence must be possible (preferably via referenda).

166886.explanation: Local taxes may not increase; PVV wants to reduce property tax and other levies as much as possible where feasible.

166887.explanation: Cooperation can be useful if it reduces costs and improves services, but not if it reduces local decision-making and proximity for residents. Nuance is therefore necessary!

166888.explanation: PVV supports the agricultural sector and wants no extra local restrictions; stop nitrogen and regulatory excesses that hinder farmers.

166891.explanation: Recreation and tourism that fits the countryside can contribute to the economy and livability. However, it is essential that it is small scale and fits the village character.

166893.explanation: Preserving farmland is part of supporting farmers and the countryside; PVV opposes unnecessary conversions to nature mandated from above. Especially since extra nature often also leads to additional nitrogen restrictions for the immediate area.

166895.explanation: PVV opposes municipal subsidies for ‘green’ energy projects; prefers reliable energy (gas/nuclear) and no climate subsidies. It is of course always up to residents to install solar panels without subsidies or net metering, preferably off the grid.

166896.explanation: PVV wants reliable infrastructure with maximum protection of village and landscape quality; for new routes, nuisance to residents must be minimal, especially when Achtkarspelen itself benefits nothing from the new high-voltage grid. Underground placement must be insisted on.

166897.explanation: Fryslân is a vulnerable agricultural province where protection against wolves through fences and similar means is not feasible. The PVV wants a wolf-free Fryslân; there must be insistence on (rapid) culling possibilities.

166898.explanation: PVV wants vital shopping streets and less vacancy; concentrating shops in larger centers can contribute to accessibility and liveliness.

166899.explanation: PVV wants to stop climate/sustainability regulations and ideological subsidies, where the bill ultimately always ends up with the citizen one way or another. Stop climate deceit. Focus on basic services and affordability. See our election program for more information.

166900.explanation: PVV does not want municipal subsidies for sustainability measures (such as insulation), not even selectively; measures should be voluntary and without municipal subsidies. And if it is deemed absolutely necessary, then for everyone!

166901.explanation: PVV opposes wind turbines (including small ones) because of the landscape and costs that (indirectly) flow back to society; no wind projects on industrial estates. No longer cooperate with weather-dependent power generation that totally disrupts our energy grid and pushes our country further into poverty.

166902.explanation: PVV wants to cooperate with social initiatives that enhance livability and safety; community initiatives with concrete and measurable effects deserve support, provided they truly add value for a large number of people.

166903.explanation: PVV is against solar fields; solar panels on agricultural land harm the landscape and agricultural function and contribute, through weather-dependent power generation, to rising energy costs and instability of the power grid. See our election program for more information.

166904.explanation: Accessible basic facilities belong to a livable municipality; more suitable public toilets help residents and visitors.

166905.explanation: No further deterioration or insecurity should occur. Maintenance is already often insufficient in many places. Therefore, consistently maintain green spaces.

166906.explanation: PVV opposes 'woke' and symbolic policies; no identity politics as a municipal government. Everyone already has the right to equal treatment under existing legislation. That is sufficient. Also point out which groups in our society typically cause discrimination.

166907.explanation: Homes are primarily for local residents; no priority for status holders in social rental housing. See our election program for more information.

166908.explanation: PVV wants to reduce existing shelter and no new shelter/housing; the municipality must resist asylum shelter to the maximum extent.

166909.explanation: PVV does not want (additional) integration and civic integration projects funded by the municipality. The municipality should focus on encouraging newcomers, as soon as possible, to return to their country or region of origin. The municipality's focus should be on its own residents and core tasks.

166910.explanation: If labor migration/seasonal work occurs, the responsibility for housing primarily belongs with employers; no shifting of this burden onto villages and the housing market.

166911.explanation: PVV supports talent development, which includes music lessons. But universally free offerings from the municipality for “other creative subjects” are too unclear for us.

166912.explanation: PVV wants to preserve facilities in villages; small schools are important for livability and families. However, preserving very small schools must not come at the expense of quality education, especially when villages are located close together. Tailored solutions are necessary.

166913.explanation: One location/broad facility can reduce bureaucracy and relieve parents; PVV supports practical solutions in education and childcare.

166914.explanation: PVV wants to support sports clubs, but realistically, uniformly, and proportionally; the municipality can help, but full takeover of all insurances is unnecessary.

166915.explanation: Education about healthy eating is fine, but structural municipal funding for school gardens is not a core task; better to focus on basic skills and sports/physical activity.

166916.explanation: PVV wants recreation close to home and explicitly mentions sport fishing; more accessible fishing spots for people with disabilities fit this vision.

166917.explanation: PVV wants facilities nearby and physical accessibility (counters/services); a lending point in each village helps accessibility and literacy.

166918.explanation: PVV wants broad support for the Frisian language and traditions, including Frisian-language education. We oppose English-language education in primary schools. For too long, there's been a mentality of 'away with our culture and language' in our country. The Anglicization, even up to university education, has gone completely overboard.

166919.explanation: PVV wants vital villages with basic facilities; schools, village halls, and sports fields directly contribute to livability and community, provided that forcing their presence in every village does not harm the quality of the facilities.

166920.explanation: Cybercrime affects residents and entrepreneurs; prevention and education fit within PVV's safety agenda.

166921.explanation: PVV wants to encourage meeting places for youth as much as possible.

166922.explanation: PVV primarily wants more police capacity. If that is not achievable after all possible efforts, then additional deployment of public order officers (BOA) is an option.

166924.explanation: PVV wants prevention and combating alcohol and drug problems among youth; education is part of this.

166925.explanation: PVV wants to deploy camera surveillance at risk locations and industrial estates as part of safety policy, but only where necessary, to safeguard privacy as much as possible.

166926.explanation: PVV supports Frisian language and customs; official Frisian village names fit with preserving identity.

166927.explanation: PVV wants to accelerate housing development (including plot splitting/caretaker homes), which also includes building homes on one’s own property.

166929.explanation: PVV wants priority for local residents in (social) rental and new construction; this prevents displacement and ensures the housing shortage problem for local residents does not persist endlessly in Achtkarspelen.

166930.explanation: PVV wants affordable homes for local residents and flow-through, but does not impose fixed quota percentages; the needs of local residents and local circumstances should be leading.

166931.explanation: PVV opposes climate 'hysteria' but wants practical protection against flooding and damage; sober tailored solutions instead of costly and unnecessary climate agendas. See our election program for more information.

166932.explanation: PVV opts for less government and less bureaucracy; social rental housing is primarily the responsibility of corporations/market, the municipality should mainly improve conditions and reduce regulatory pressure.

166933.explanation: PVV wants to prevent homes from being bought up for investment/rental; owner-occupancy obligation helps affordability and availability for local residents. However, it must remain possible, for example, for parents to buy a home for a child.

166934.explanation: PVV wants less bureaucracy and to remove unnecessary rules; aesthetic regulations can be simplified or abolished to speed up building.

166935.explanation: Tackling vacancy and using existing buildings fits with sober spatial use; conversion to housing can quickly provide additional supply.

166936.explanation: PVV wants to tackle poverty and stress, including through tax relief and targeted support, without leveling down. In other words, lower costs for everyone.

166937.explanation: PVV stands for responsibility and activation; those who can should make a social contribution in exchange for benefits.

166938.explanation: PVV wants to get people into work and provide perspective, but ‘many more subsidized jobs’ is not necessarily the solution; focus on tax relief, real jobs, fewer rules, and involving employers. By the way, getting people with disabilities into work is a task of the UWV.

166939.explanation: PVV wants activation; an obligation to apply for jobs is part of this, but above all making it attractive for people to actually start applying by ensuring they have more left at the end of the day.

166940.explanation: More room to earn extra income stimulates work and outflow; PVV wants to strengthen incentives to work.

166942.explanation: PVV is against expensive prestige projects and wants money directed to basics and safety; a new navigable lake is not a priority.

166944.explanation: PVV wants good lighting and safe situations; lighting of bicycle paths contributes to the safety of students and residents.

166945.explanation: PVV opposes the climate/electrification agenda and additional 'green' facilities; increasing parking pressure and sacrificing public space for more charging stations is undesirable. Especially if this is paid for by our citizens who can hardly or not use them at all.

166947.explanation: PVV supports safe bicycle paths, but potentially fast intercity bicycle routes to big cities are a provincial responsibility; our priority lies with local safety and accessibility.

166949.explanation: Better public transport accessibility is important; a stop in Buitenpost can contribute to accessibility, provided it is feasible in the timetable.

166950.explanation: PVV wants a strong community and appreciation for informal caregivers and volunteers; extra efforts to stimulate informal care fit this.

166951.explanation: PVV wants meeting places and caring villages; a meeting place for elderly people in each village helps against loneliness and strengthens community spirit.

166952.explanation: PVV wants a focus on real prevention by primarily preventing problems where possible. Early detection and cooperation between schools and youth care is necessary and can help prevent worse situations where prevention has failed.

166954.explanation: PVV wants care for those who need it and to reduce burdens; at the same time, we oppose equalization.

166955.explanation: PVV wants to shorten waiting times by prioritizing and making processes as efficient as possible. However, we also believe that money spent on youth care should be closely examined.

166886.explanation: VVD opposes increasing the tax burden.

166887.explanation: Where possible, the municipality of Achtkarspelen should cooperate. This is to maintain the quality and costs of services.

166888.explanation: Farmers hold an important place in our municipality. Where possible, we support farmers; expanding intensive farming is not our preference.

166891.explanation: Achtkarspelen is a beautiful region where we can do much more with tourism.

166893.explanation: We do not want to put unnecessary pressure on agriculture.

166895.explanation: There are various other funds available to finance such initiatives.

166896.explanation: As a municipality, we cannot stop this. VVD wants to commit to a good compensation scheme.

166899.explanation: This is actually ideal for people with a smaller budget.

166900.explanation: VVD wants to support people with an income up to 120% of the social minimum in making sustainable improvements.

166901.explanation: As long as no nuisance is experienced by neighbors.

166902.explanation: Citizens can organize this perfectly well themselves.

166903.explanation: VVD prefers to first investigate other locations, such as rooftops.

166905.explanation: Good for biodiversity, provided it does not lead to unsafe situations.

166907.explanation: There is a severe housing shortage; we want to treat all urgent cases equally.

166908.explanation: We recognize the problems that come with asylum seeker reception. The type of reception must fit within the municipality of Achtkarspelen. Active resistance goes too far for VVD.

166909.explanation: Afûk is already addressing this on behalf of the province.

166911.explanation: Through poverty policy, we stimulate children living under the social minimum.

166912.explanation: There must be a minimum number of pupils to keep a school open. Quality must remain guaranteed.

166913.explanation: Where possible, this is a good solution, but it should not be an obligation for VVD.

166914.explanation: All associations must be treated equally. The premise is that an association is self-sufficient.

166915.explanation: A vegetable garden is fine, but students and teachers can maintain this vegetable garden themselves.

166916.explanation: There are already sufficient places where people with disabilities can fish.

166917.explanation: We want to explore whether something like this can be arranged through, for example, primary schools.

166918.explanation: We hold the Frisian language dearly.

166919.explanation: This can vary per village; however, the size must be large enough.

166920.explanation: This is already a nationwide campaign.

166921.explanation: As long as it does not cause any nuisance and is not for commercial purposes.

166922.explanation: The VVD favors more police officers instead of enforcement officers (BOAs).

166924.explanation: This must be done in good consultation with the schools.

166925.explanation: Only when necessary at locations where there is a lot of nuisance.

166926.explanation: Given the extremely high costs for such a change, the VVD disagrees with this statement.

166927.explanation: This depends greatly on the situation. In rural areas, we see more possibilities than in village centers.

166930.explanation: Mandating a minimum number of social rental homes creates additional regulatory pressure. We need to look at what is needed per village.

166932.explanation: The SWA has already been established for this purpose.

166933.explanation: Homes are built to live in, not for investment purposes.

166934.explanation: The VVD supports reducing regulatory pressure.

166935.explanation: This is the responsibility of the owner. However, we want to ease the regulations.

166936.explanation: The VVD supports continuation of the current policy.

166939.explanation: We follow the national regulations in this matter.

166940.explanation: We follow the national regulations in this matter.

166945.explanation: Residents who own an electric car often already have their own charging station.

166947.explanation: We promote good and safe infrastructure.

166949.explanation: The region must remain easily accessible.

166950.explanation: This is not a core task of the municipality.

166951.explanation: This is already organized by the community itself (mienskip).

166952.explanation: We want the youth to have a fair chance.

166955.explanation: The VVD wants a good price/quality ratio.

166884.explanation: Advisory referendum, only in exceptional situations.

166886.explanation: The outcome, that is what we do with it, must be visible to our residents.

166887.explanation: Only when we cannot do it alone or when it adds value.

166888.explanation: Relatively a lot in Achtkarspelen; prefer to scale down rather than expand.

166891.explanation: Achtkarspelen has a lot to offer. Focusing on recreation and tourism offers opportunities.

166893.explanation: Farmers are important for Achtkarspelen and are part of the community (mienskip). The modern farmer is also a nature protector.

166895.explanation: We believe the municipality should support residents’ initiatives with advice and assistance. This can also be financially.

166896.explanation: They have to be placed somewhere, and if objective research shows that it is the best location in Achtkarspelen, we should cooperate.

166897.explanation: Only in cases of extreme nuisance and when no other solutions are possible.

166898.explanation: Strong shopping centers add value to the entire municipality. We also support preserving shops in small villages.

166899.explanation: Sustainability is not only an environmental issue but also contributes to poverty reduction and housing quality.

166900.explanation: Everyone has the right to sustainable living. The primary responsibility lies with the resident. Residents who cannot afford it themselves should be helped by the municipality.

166901.explanation: Of course, in good coordination with the neighbors.

166902.explanation: We believe the municipality should support residents’ initiatives; this can include financial support.

166903.explanation: Not preferred on agricultural land; strictly prohibiting this goes too far. First, consider other alternatives such as rooftops.

166904.explanation: This also belongs to an inclusive society.

166905.explanation: Current policy is already focused on limited mowing.

166906.explanation: Part of an inclusive society, good to be inviting in that.

166907.explanation: Provided that priority is not reserved only for refugees. Prefer sustainable integration over large-scale shelter. The task of sheltering status holders is not a choice but national policy.

166908.explanation: Asylum policy is a national/European issue. As municipalities, we must take responsibility together. We prefer sustainable integration over large-scale temporary shelter.

166909.explanation: Integration is very important and can receive much more attention, but not specifically regarding Achtkarspelen.

166910.explanation: Employers must provide housing for their seasonal workers. We consider good housing more important than housing on their own premises. But it is the responsibility of employers to ensure this.

166911.explanation: Talent development should receive more attention. Money should not be a barrier preventing any child from getting the opportunity.

166912.explanation: Is not the responsibility of the municipality. Focus on cooperative schools, maintaining quality foremost. However, do everything possible to preserve livability in all villages.

166913.explanation: Supporter of the broad school model.

166914.explanation: Responsibility lies with the association. The municipality can financially support associations if necessary.

166915.explanation: Initiatives like these are appreciated but the responsibility lies with the schools.

166916.explanation: Support an inclusive society but broader than just fishing.

166917.explanation: In schools or village halls. Literacy is extremely important.

166918.explanation: Is good for the development of children.

166919.explanation: Especially important for young children to be able to attend school or be actively engaged in their immediate surroundings.

166920.explanation: Is a major issue with many risks needing more attention.

166921.explanation: It is important that young people have their own place, but safety is also important.

166922.explanation: Prefer to invest in youth workers (prevention) rather than enforcement. Prevention is better than cure.

166924.explanation: Alcohol and drug use is high in Achtkarspelen. The damage can be enormous and permanent. With good education, we might prevent this.

166926.explanation: Yes, while retaining the Dutch names.

166927.explanation: Explicitly offer the possibility, but in consultation.

166929.explanation: Priority based on urgency must remain possible.

166930.explanation: Percentage is not decisive. The municipality must build enough social rental housing to meet demand because housing is a basic right. That right applies to everyone, so also ensure sufficient housing for other groups.

166931.explanation: We have seen too many extremes in recent years, also in Achtkarspelen.

166932.explanation: Housing construction must receive the highest priority, not fully dependent on others.

166933.explanation: With the major shortages in the housing market, housing must be the primary objective and not investment.

166934.explanation: Should critically examine the usefulness and necessity.

166935.explanation: Not financially, but promote combating vacancy and encourage housing.

166936.explanation: No one should live in poverty in a prosperous country like the Netherlands.

166940.explanation: Is a step towards work and contributes to combating poverty.

166944.explanation: Everyone must be able to move safely throughout the municipality.

166950.explanation: Although money is not or should not be a motivation to care for each other, we should embrace and facilitate it.

166951.explanation: For everyone, not just the elderly.

166884.explanation: Residents deserve a voice in major decisions. BBB wants the municipality to enable referendums on issues that affect everyone, with clear rules and good information beforehand. This way, we measure support and increase trust. The council remains the decision-maker but with the right information.

166886.explanation: The property tax is already a significant burden for many families and entrepreneurs. BBB does not want to drive housing costs any higher. First, prioritize more sharply, purchase smarter, and prevent waste.

166887.explanation: Cooperating with other municipalities can be useful, but BBB is cautious. Achtkarspelen has recently undergone disentanglement and residents want calm and clarity first. BBB believes the municipality should take responsibility itself again and be visible to its residents.

166888.explanation: BBB does not want a general cap on expansion. Agriculture is important for jobs, food, and the countryside. Growth must fit the location and environment: traffic, nature, and health all count. Therefore, we choose customized solutions and permits based on facts, not sentiment.

166891.explanation: Tourism can bring extra income for hospitality and shops, but it must fit the tranquility and spaciousness of our villages. BBB chooses targeted investment in quality: routes, nature experiences, and good facilities, not expensive promotion without results or nuisance.

166893.explanation: Good agricultural land is scarce and should remain available for food production and agricultural futures. BBB wants to prevent land from being too easily converted to nature projects without support. If conversion occurs, it must be done carefully, with compensation, and in good consultation with landowners.

166895.explanation: If residents want to generate energy together, such as a cooperative with solar panels on roofs, that deserves support. BBB chooses local ownership and local benefits. Subsidies should be simple and targeted, mainly helping projects that fit the village and truly lower energy bills.

166896.explanation: BBB wants to protect our landscape and living environment, but we also need a reliable power grid. The municipality must be critical, consider alternatives (bundling along infrastructure, underground where possible) and set strict conditions. Opposing ‘always’ doesn’t work; steering towards the best solution does.

166897.explanation: When the wolf causes problems with livestock or safety, action must be taken quickly. BBB wants the municipality to pressure the province for clear rules, rapid compensation, and intervention where necessary.

166898.explanation: BBB believes basic facilities should remain as close as possible to residents. Not everyone lives in larger villages and not everyone can travel easily. Small villages also deserve facilities to remain livable. Therefore, BBB chooses spreading out where possible and customized solutions per village.

166899.explanation: Stopping all sustainability schemes is too simplistic for BBB. We want practical measures that pay off for residents and entrepreneurs: insulation, energy saving, and local generation. But we are critical of expensive symbolic projects. So: continue, but soberly, measurably, and proven effective.

166900.explanation: BBB wants to help especially those who need it, but 'low income’ alone is too limited. Middle incomes also face difficulties with high insulation costs. Therefore: support aimed at impact and capacity to pay, with a good mix of subsidies/loans and clear conditions.

166901.explanation: Small wind turbines can fit on business parks but must be safe and not cause nuisance (noise, shadow flicker). BBB is open to pilots with clear rules and good measurements.

166902.explanation: Strong villages arise from people wanting to organize something. BBB wants to support initiatives for community spirit: village activities, meetings, sports, and volunteering. With simple application and small budgets quickly available. This strengthens the 'mienskip' without much paperwork.

166903.explanation: BBB wants to preserve agricultural land for food production. Land is scarce and primarily belongs to farms. Solar panels can be placed on roofs, homesteads, and other suitable spots. Sustainability must not come at the expense of good farmland.

166904.explanation: BBB wants people with disabilities to be able to go out independently; more accessible toilets help with that. The municipality can support shops and facilities in opening their toilets, with clear agreements about use and maintenance.

166905.explanation: BBB opposes a general rule to mow less. Less mowing can sometimes be good, but must not lead to messy or unsafe situations. Each location should be considered individually. Residents should also be able to easily contact the municipality if problems arise.

166906.explanation: BBB believes everyone in Achtkarspelen must be able to safely be themselves. The label ‘rainbow municipality’ is not our goal; it’s about what you do. We support concrete measures against discrimination and bullying, and good support where needed, without it becoming mainly symbolic politics.

166907.explanation: BBB believes scarce housing should primarily be available to people from our own municipality. Status holders must be decently housed but not systematically ahead of residents. We want fair, transparent rules and above all more building to relieve pressure.

166908.explanation: BBB wants Achtkarspelen to stand up for its residents. The municipality must clarify what is achievable and resist plans that overload our centers. At the same time, we must handle statutory tasks properly. So: negotiate firmly, set boundaries, and ensure fair distribution.

166909.explanation: Those who move here must quickly find their way in Achtkarspelen: how do schools, associations, work, and rules function? BBB supports a local integration module focusing on language, norms, traditions, and participation in society. This helps newcomers and prevents misunderstandings in neighborhoods.

166910.explanation: Employers who deploy labor migrants must also take responsibility for good housing. This prevents pressure on the housing market and nuisance in residential areas. BBB wants clear requirements, proper enforcement, and decent living conditions. This is fair for residents and workers alike.
