166911.explanation: BBB values talent development, but not every child needs creative lessons. Free offerings for everyone are costly. BBB chooses voluntary and affordable offerings, together with schools and associations, with attention to all talents. Creative, athletic, technical, language, and more.

166912.explanation: Primary schools are the heart of a village. When a school closes, depopulation often follows. Therefore, BBB wants to focus on keeping small schools open where possible, with quality as the starting point. Nearby education keeps villages livable and attractive for young families.

166913.explanation: A broad school can help families by bundling childcare, care, and education. BBB is positive if this improves quality and does not become an unnecessary mega-structure. We want customized solutions per center: sometimes one site is convenient, sometimes cooperation across multiple locations works better.

166914.explanation: Sports clubs are indispensable for health, social contact, and cohesion in villages. BBB wants to support them but not regulate everything centrally. Preferably tailored and targeted support so clubs remain strong and independent and sports remain affordable for everyone.

166915.explanation: A school garden teaches children where food comes from and encourages healthy eating. BBB wants to assist schools with a small contribution or materials, preferably together with local farmers or volunteers. It is practical education that fits our region and needs little expense.

166916.explanation: People with disabilities must also be able to enjoy the outdoors and water. BBB wants more accessible fishing spots with safe piers and good paths. This is a concrete improvement to recreation and inclusion, often achievable with relatively small adjustments in public spaces.

166917.explanation: Reading and digital skills are important, especially for children and the elderly. Where possible, a lending point is certainly desirable. BBB primarily wants library services nearby: via service points, schools, village halls, or a mobile library. Quality and accessibility come first.

166918.explanation: BBB stands for preserving Frisian and good opportunities for children. Trilingual education can help: strong Dutch, living Frisian, and practical English. Schools must be able to organize it well and involve parents. The municipality can stimulate and support, without making it compulsory.

166919.explanation: BBB believes villages remain livable only if basic facilities are nearby. Therefore, BBB advocates for a school, village hall, and sports field in every village. That is the starting point. If that is not possible, the municipality must explain well and seek full alternatives together with the village.

166920.explanation: Fraud and hacks affect more and more people and businesses. BBB wants the municipality to help with practical information: recognize phishing, protect your banking, and use good passwords. Especially for SMEs and the elderly, this can prevent much damage. Prevention over repair.

166921.explanation: BBB understands young people want a place, but safety and neighbors also matter. An unauthorized hut can lead to fire hazards, nuisance, and alcohol problems. We want to offer space: make it possible with clear, feasible rules and a quick permit. Trust is the starting point.

166922.explanation: Safety is important, but more enforcement officers are not always the solution. BBB first wants to identify the problem: youth, traffic, nuisance? Sometimes more enforcement helps, sometimes better lighting or neighborhood contacts. If extra officers are needed, deploy them targeted at hotspots and visibility.

166924.explanation: Early education helps prevent problems. BBB wants practical information at schools and through associations, focusing on alcohol, vaping, and drugs. Not preachy, but honest about consequences. Combine this with good cooperation between parents, youth work, and enforcement where needed.

166925.explanation: Cameras can help tackle nuisance and investigations, but also affect privacy. BBB wants camera surveillance only where truly necessary, with clear goals, temporary use, and good agreements. Prevention and visible presence on the street remain the basis.

166926.explanation: BBB attaches great value to the Frisian language and culture and wants to keep the language alive. Changing official village names adds little and entails extra costs. The current situation works well: Frisian names are used and remain visible without extra administrative burdens.

166927.explanation: BBB wants young people and informal caregivers to be able to live close to their family. Temporary homes on their own property can help. Going entirely without rules goes too far: safety, sewage, and neighbors must be taken into account. Therefore, the rules should be simplified and decisions made faster.

166929.explanation: In case of scarcity, it is logical to first consider people with ties to Achtkarspelen: young people, families, and people who work or provide informal care here. BBB wants clear rules for local priority within legal possibilities. This way, we keep our villages livable for our own residents.

166930.explanation: BBB wants more affordable housing, but a fixed percentage of 40% can actually slow down construction in some locations. We want to guide based on the needs of each village: social rental where the shortage is large, but also room for starter purchase and mid-level rent. So: strong focus on affordability, with customized solutions per plan.

166931.explanation: Heavy rain and heat occur more often. BBB wants new construction to be practically adapted for this: good drainage, enough greenery, shade, and space for water. This prevents damage and extra costs afterwards. Not with expensive prestige projects, but with smart designs that work in practice.

166932.explanation: BBB mainly wants more affordable housing quickly. A municipal housing corporation is not feasible with all its risks, costs, and associated organization. We choose collaboration with housing corporations, speeding up procedures, and smart use of land.

166933.explanation: Homes are for living in, not for speculation. BBB supports a self-occupancy obligation to curb investors and give starters a fairer chance. This helps affordability and prevents villages from being 'locked down' due to high prices and vacancy. We also welcome fines for vacant properties.

166934.explanation: BBB wants less nannying, but building must be safe and fit the environment. Completely abolishing regulations can lead to messy situations or neighborhood disputes. We do want to simplify aesthetic rules: less taste policing, more clear basic agreements, and faster decisions.

166935.explanation: Vacant commercial buildings offer opportunities to create housing faster, especially for starters and small households. BBB wants the municipality to actively facilitate this: thinking along, speeding up permits, and giving a push where needed. This addresses vacancy and increases the housing supply.

166936.explanation: No one should fall between the cracks. BBB wants help with poverty to be easily accessible: early detection, effective debt assistance, and support with energy and housing costs. People who just exceed the limits must also be able to get help. Practical support, no piles of forms.

166937.explanation: BBB values participation. A reciprocal obligation can help build routine, contacts, and self-worth. It must fit a person’s abilities and never feel like a punishment. Think of volunteer work, neighborhood jobs, or support at clubs, with good guidance and customized approach.

166938.explanation: BBB wants people to work, but subsidized jobs must lead to real progression and not be endless. We choose guidance towards regular jobs, practical learning, and cooperation with local employers. Where a wage cost subsidy helps hire someone, we are open to that.

166939.explanation: Those who can work must actively take steps. BBB wants the municipality to take the application obligation seriously again, with clear agreements and good guidance. At the same time, people with disabilities or care tasks deserve customization. The goal is that more residents participate again and earn their own income.

166940.explanation: Earning extra can actually help make the step towards work smaller. BBB wants more room to temporarily earn extra without immediate deductions. That makes working worthwhile and gives people a chance to gain experience and build a network again. But with clear boundaries and enforceable rules.

166942.explanation: BBB considers this an unrealistic plan. It is expensive, drastic, and brings risks for nature, water, and the environment. The added value for Achtkarspelen is unclear. BBB prefers investments that directly contribute to livability, economy, and nature.

166944.explanation: Lighting increases safety, especially in winter. BBB therefore wants smart choices: lighting where it is really needed (school routes, busy connections) and working with energy-efficient or dimmable systems. Safe and responsible.

166945.explanation: Electric driving is growing, so sufficient charging points are needed. BBB wants this to grow with demand, without unnecessarily increasing parking pressure. First look at smart locations near facilities and village centers. Not a goal in itself, but ensuring residents and entrepreneurs can use it practically.

166947.explanation: Good cycling routes are important, but 'fast connections' to big cities are expensive and sometimes beyond our influence. BBB wants safe, well-maintained routes within the municipality and good connections to regional networks. If there are subsidies and it really adds value, we will participate.

166949.explanation: Buitenpost is an important station for the residents of Achtkarspelen. It would be nice if more express trains stop here, but that is not a goal in itself. BBB wants to look together with Arriva at what is possible and feasible, without losing sight of the reliability of public transport.

166950.explanation: Community ('Mienskip') is a core value for BBB. In villages, families, and churches, people already do a lot for each other. That effort is indispensable. BBB wants practical help from the municipality, such as informal care support, temporary replacement care, one clear counter, and fewer rules. So people are not left alone.

166951.explanation: A meeting place prevents loneliness, keeps elderly vital longer, and gives meaning to life. BBB wants every village to have access to such a place; in a village hall or at a sports canteen. Not always a new building, but a fixed place and activities, with support for volunteers.

166952.explanation: Early detection of problems prevents heavier youth care later. BBB wants more preventive support in and around schools: youth help nearby, short lines with parents, and quick referrals. That helps children earlier and saves money and misery in the long run.

166954.explanation: BBB believes the personal contribution should be fair. One fixed amount for everyone sounds simple but often hits low incomes harder. We want a scheme that takes ability to pay into account, with clear rates and as little bureaucracy as possible.

166955.explanation: In youth care, quality and reliability are paramount, not the lowest price. BBB wants the municipality to focus on proven results, continuity, and cooperation with families and schools. Of course, it must be affordable, but 'price first' often leads to worse care and higher costs later.

statements.title: The municipality should oppose the expansion of intensive livestock farming.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet uitbreiding van de

titleParts.glossary: Intensive livestock farms where animals such as pigs, chickens and calves are kept on a relatively small area.

statements.title: Shops should, as much as possible, be concentrated together in the largest villages.
The municipality must oppose the installation of high-voltage pylons in Achtkarspelen.
Residents who want to generate sustainable energy together should receive financial support from the municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Energy based on resources that are effectively infinite, such as sunlight and wind.

titleParts.text: op willen wekken, moeten financiële steun krijgen van de gemeente.

statements.title: The municipality must spend money to keep primary schools with few pupils open.
The municipality must, as much as possible, oppose accommodating asylum seekers.

titleParts.text: De gemeente moet zich zoveel mogelijk verzetten tegen het opvangen van

titleParts.glossary: People who have applied for asylum and are still waiting for a decision.

statements.title: Young people in Achtkarspelen may open their own youth shack without the municipality's permission.
The residents of Achtkarspelen must be able to give their opinion on important topics via a referendum.

titleParts.text: De inwoners van Achtkarspelen moeten over belangrijke onderwerpen hun mening kunnen geven via een

titleParts.glossary: A public consultation where residents can express their opinion on a political issue. The municipal council is not obliged to follow the result; legally it cannot.

statements.title: The municipality must ensure that each village has a school, a village hall and a sports field.
There must be more municipal enforcement officers (BOAs) in Achtkarspelen who focus on safety.

titleParts.glossary: People who, for the municipality, check whether citizens and businesses comply with the rules.

titleParts.text: (boa’s) komen die zich met veiligheid bezighouden.

statements.title: Only people with a low income must receive money from the municipality to make their homes more energy-efficient.
Residents of Achtkarspelen may build temporary homes on their own property without the municipality's permission.

statements.theme: Earning extra income while receiving social assistance
statements.title: People on social assistance must be given more freedom to earn extra income.

titleParts.glossary: Money from the municipality for people who have no income or personal funds.

titleParts.text: moeten meer ruimte krijgen om bij te verdienen.

statements.title: The municipality must do less itself and cooperate more with other municipalities.
The municipality must abolish the aesthetic standards for building and renovating.

titleParts.glossary: Municipal rules that determine how the houses and gardens in a neighbourhood should look.

statements.title: The municipality should designate more parking spaces as charging points for electric cars.
All bike paths in Achtkarspelen must be lit.
The municipality must give primary schools funding to create and maintain a vegetable garden.
The municipality should ban solar panels on agricultural land.
Achtkarspelen must build fast cycling connections to Leeuwarden, Groningen, Drachten and Dokkum.
People within village communities and churches take care of one another. The municipality must spend more money to encourage that care.
Anyone who buys a home in our municipality must live in it themselves.
Every primary school child must be able to take free after-school lessons in music and other creative subjects.
The co-payment for domestic help must be the same for everyone.
The municipality must start building and renting its own social housing.

titleParts.glossary: Rental homes that at the start of the tenancy are not more expensive than a certain amount. In 2025 that amount was €900.07 per month.

statements.title: The municipality must invest in converting vacant commercial buildings into housing.
There must be many more subsidized jobs for the long-term unemployed in our municipality.

titleParts.glossary: Government-funded jobs for people who have little chance of getting a regular job.
People who have been without paid work for a long time (longer than a year).

statements.title: The municipality must prevent agricultural land in Achtkarspelen from being converted into nature areas.
Children must be able to access education, childcare and after-school activities on one site from birth through primary school.
The Frisian village names must become the official names in Achtkarspelen.

shootoutStatements.title: The municipality must continue to give refugees with a residence permit priority for social housing.
Each village should have a library lending point.
People receiving social assistance should, in return, do socially useful work.
More public toilets for people with disabilities should be installed in Achtkarspelen.
The municipality should run an awareness campaign to warn residents and businesses about cybercrime.
Achtkarspelen should become a rainbow municipality.
The municipality should pay the insurance premiums of sports clubs.
The municipality should require newcomers to take a local integration course about Achtkarspelen.
The municipality should allow small wind turbines on business parks.
The municipality should help more residents who are in poverty.
Property tax (ozb) must not be increased.
The municipality should mow public grass less often.
Achtkarspelen should invest more money in promoting tourism.
The municipality should press Arriva to have every express train stop in Buitenpost.
Camera surveillance should be introduced in Achtkarspelen.
Every village in the municipality should have its own meeting place for elderly people.
The municipality should reinstate the requirement that people on social assistance actively apply for jobs.
The municipality should provide more education to young people about the dangers of alcohol and drugs.
The municipality should spend more money on preventive youth care in schools.
A navigable lake should be created at the Wide Pet, between Harkema and Surhuisterveen.
When selecting organizations for youth care, the municipality should primarily pay attention to price.
The municipality should make funds available for citizens with plans that strengthen cohesion in the community.
If a wolf causes problems, the municipality should urge the province to have it shot.
Employers in agriculture and horticulture should provide housing for seasonal workers on their own premises.
The municipality should stop sustainability programs.
At least 40% of newly built homes in Achtkarspelen should be social-rental housing; currently 28% of homes in the Netherlands are social-rental.
There should be more fishing spots for people with disabilities.
The municipality should ensure new development sites are designed for extreme weather like heatwaves or heavy rain.
The municipality should encourage primary schools to become trilingual schools, teaching children in Dutch, Frisian and English.
When allocating social housing, applicants from the municipality should be given priority.
